  Manages LDAP configuration for Pocket-ID. This resource allows you to configure LDAP/Active Directory integration for user and group synchronization.

# pocketid_ldap_config (Resource)

Manages LDAP configuration for Pocket-ID. This resource allows you to configure LDAP/Active Directory integration for user and group synchronization.

~> **Note** Setting enabled to false effectively "deletes" the LDAP configuration. The bind_password is sensitive and will not be shown in logs or plan output.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Basic LDAP configuration with minimal attributes
resource "pocketid_ldap_config" "example" {
  enabled = true

  # Connection settings
  url            = "ldap://ldap.example.com:389"
  bind_dn        = "cn=admin,dc=example,dc=com"
  bind_password  = "admin-password"
  base_dn        = "dc=example,dc=com"

  # User attribute mappings (required when enabled)
  user_attributes {
    unique_identifier = "objectGUID"
    username          = "sAMAccountName"
  }
}

# Full LDAP configuration with Active Directory
resource "pocketid_ldap_config" "active_directory" {
  enabled = true

  # Connection settings
  url              = "ldaps://ad.company.com:636"
  bind_dn          = "CN=LDAP Reader,OU=Service Accounts,DC=company,DC=com"
  bind_password    = var.ldap_bind_password
  base_dn          = "DC=company,DC=com"
  skip_cert_verify = false  # Use true for self-signed certificates

  # Search filters
  user_search_filter       = "(&(objectClass=user)(objectCategory=person))"
  user_group_search_filter = "(objectClass=group)"

  # User synchronization behavior
  soft_delete_users = true  # Disable users not found in LDAP instead of deleting

  # User attribute mappings
  user_attributes {
    unique_identifier = "objectGUID"
    username          = "sAMAccountName"
    email             = "mail"
    first_name        = "givenName"
    last_name         = "sn"
    profile_picture   = "thumbnailPhoto"
  }

  # Group attribute mappings
  group_attributes {
    member            = "member"
    unique_identifier = "objectGUID"
    name              = "cn"
    admin_group_name  = "PocketID-Admins"  # Members of this group become admins
  }
}

# OpenLDAP configuration example
resource "pocketid_ldap_config" "openldap" {
  enabled = true

  # Connection settings
  url           = "ldap://openldap.example.com:389"
  bind_dn       = "cn=readonly,dc=example,dc=com"
  bind_password = var.ldap_password
  base_dn       = "dc=example,dc=com"

  # OpenLDAP specific search filters
  user_search_filter       = "(objectClass=inetOrgPerson)"
  user_group_search_filter = "(objectClass=groupOfNames)"

  # User attributes for OpenLDAP
  user_attributes {
    unique_identifier = "entryUUID"
    username          = "uid"
    email             = "mail"
    first_name        = "givenName"
    last_name         = "sn"
  }

  # Group attributes
  group_attributes {
    member            = "member"
    unique_identifier = "entryUUID"
    name              = "cn"
  }
}

# Disable LDAP configuration
resource "pocketid_ldap_config" "disabled" {
  enabled = false
}

# Use with LDAP sync resource
resource "pocketid_ldap_config" "with_sync" {
  enabled = true

  url           = "ldap://ldap.example.com:389"
  bind_dn       = "cn=admin,dc=example,dc=com"
  bind_password = var.ldap_password
  base_dn       = "dc=example,dc=com"

  user_attributes {
    unique_identifier = "uid"
    username          = "uid"
    email             = "mail"
  }
}

# Trigger sync when LDAP config changes
resource "pocketid_ldap_sync" "auto_sync" {
  triggers = {
    config_change = pocketid_ldap_config.with_sync.id
  }
}

# Test LDAP configuration before enabling
data "pocketid_ldap_test" "test" {
  # This data source tests the current LDAP configuration
}

resource "pocketid_ldap_config" "conditional" {
  # Only enable if test passes
  enabled = data.pocketid_ldap_test.test.connection_successful

  url           = "ldap://ldap.example.com:389"
  bind_dn       = "cn=admin,dc=example,dc=com"
  bind_password = var.ldap_password
  base_dn       = "dc=example,dc=com"

  user_attributes {
    unique_identifier = "uid"
    username          = "uid"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable or disable LDAP integration. When false, all other settings are ignored by Pocket ID.

### Optional

- `base_dn` (String) Base DN for LDAP searches. Example: dc=example,dc=com.
- `bind_dn` (String) Distinguished Name for LDAP bind authentication. Example: cn=admin,dc=example,dc=com. Anonymous binding is not supported.
- `bind_password` (String, Sensitive) Password for bind DN. Anonymous binding is not supported.
- `group_attributes` (Block, Optional) LDAP attribute mappings for groups. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--group_attributes))
- `skip_cert_verify` (Boolean) Skip TLS certificate verification for LDAPS connections. Use with caution in production. Defaults to `false`.
- `soft_delete_users` (Boolean) When true, users not found in LDAP are disabled. When false, they are deleted. Defaults to `true`.
- `url` (String) LDAP server URL. Must be in format: ldap://host:port or ldaps://host:port.
- `user_attributes` (Block, Optional) LDAP attribute mappings for users. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedblock--user_attributes))
- `user_group_search_filter` (String) LDAP filter for finding groups. Defaults to `(objectClass=groupOfNames)`.
- `user_search_filter` (String) LDAP filter for finding users. Defaults to `(objectClass=person)`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of the LDAP configuration (always 'ldap').

<a id="nestedblock--group_attributes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `group_attributes`

Optional:

- `admin_group_name` (String) Name of LDAP group that grants admin role. Members of this group become Pocket ID admins.
- `member` (String) LDAP attribute for group members. Defaults to `member`.
- `name` (String) LDAP attribute for group name. Example: cn.
- `unique_identifier` (String) LDAP attribute for unique group identifier. Examples: objectGUID, entryUUID, cn.

<a id="nestedblock--user_attributes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `user_attributes`

Optional:

- `email` (String) LDAP attribute for email address. Example: mail.
- `first_name` (String) LDAP attribute for first name. Example: givenName.
- `last_name` (String) LDAP attribute for last name. Example: sn.
- `profile_picture` (String) LDAP attribute for profile picture. Can be URL, base64, or binary data. Example: thumbnailPhoto.
- `unique_identifier` (String) LDAP attribute containing unique user identifier. Examples: objectGUID, entryUUID, uid.
- `username` (String) LDAP attribute for username. Examples: sAMAccountName, uid, cn.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

```shell
# LDAP configuration can be imported using the ID "ldap"
terraform import pocketid_ldap_config.example ldap
```
